questions.question: Explain how democratic rights protect citizens from misuse of power.
questions.answer: Democratic rights limit government authority and allow citizens to challenge injustice through courts.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the Right to Equality in democracy.
questions.answer: The Right to Equality ensures equal treatment of citizens and prevents discrimination in society.

questions.question: Explain the Right to Freedom and its reasonable restrictions.
questions.answer: The Right to Freedom provides several freedoms but also includes restrictions to maintain public order and national security.

questions.question: Explain the significance of minority rights in democracy.
questions.answer: Minority rights protect cultural identity and ensure minorities are not dominated by the majority.

questions.question: Explain the Right against Exploitation with examples.
questions.answer: This right protects individuals from forced labour, child labour and human trafficking.

questions.question: Explain the role of judiciary in protecting Fundamental Rights.
questions.answer: The judiciary protects rights by issuing orders, writs and ensuring justice.

questions.question: Explain why the Right to Constitutional Remedies is called the heart and soul of the Constitution.
questions.answer: Because it allows citizens to approach courts when their Fundamental Rights are violated.

questions.question: Explain the concept of secularism in India.
questions.answer: Secularism means the state treats all religions equally and does not favor any religion.

questions.question: Explain Cultural and Educational Rights in detail.
questions.answer: These rights allow minorities to preserve their language, culture and establish educational institutions.

questions.question: Explain Public Interest Litigation with an example.
questions.answer: Public Interest Litigation allows citizens to approach courts for issues affecting public welfare.

questions.question: Explain the functions of the National Human Rights Commission.
questions.answer: NHRC investigates violations of human rights and promotes their protection.

questions.question: Explain the importance of human rights.
questions.answer: Human rights ensure dignity, equality and freedom for all individuals.

questions.question: Explain how democratic rights promote equality in society.
questions.answer: Rights ensure equal treatment and opportunities for all citizens.

questions.question: Explain the importance of freedom of speech in democracy.
questions.answer: Freedom of speech allows citizens to express opinions and criticize government policies.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between democracy and rights.
questions.answer: Rights are essential for democracy because they ensure freedom, equality and justice.

questions.question: Explain the role of education in protecting rights.
questions.answer: Education increases awareness about rights and responsibilities.

questions.question: Explain how RTI strengthens democracy.
questions.answer: RTI promotes transparency and accountability in government.

questions.question: Explain why rights expand over time.
questions.answer: Rights expand as societies evolve and new needs arise.

questions.question: Explain the importance of courts in democracy.
questions.answer: Courts protect rights and ensure justice in society.

questions.question: Explain why minority rights are essential in democracy.
questions.answer: Minority rights prevent discrimination and ensure equality.

questions.question: Explain human trafficking and its prohibition.
questions.answer: Human trafficking refers to buying and selling of people and is prohibited by law.

questions.question: Explain the importance of democratic rights for citizens.
questions.answer: Democratic rights ensure freedom, equality and dignity for citizens.

questions.question: Explain the importance of legal protection of rights.
questions.answer: Legal protection ensures that rights can be enforced through courts.

questions.question: Explain the role of the Constitution in protecting rights.
questions.answer: The Constitution guarantees Fundamental Rights and provides mechanisms for their enforcement.

questions.question: Explain how democratic rights strengthen democracy.
questions.answer: They protect citizens, promote equality and ensure justice in society.
